candidates.stmt:
Coming from an Army family, service runs in my blood – I now want to serve Abbey Wood. As a young person living in our borough I am passionate about creating opportunity and have spent time as a teacher for children learning English. In my day job, I work in communications for the public sector and businesses, where I have campaigned to widen access to housing, tackle antisocial behaviour, and lower energy bills.

As your Labour candidates we want to keep getting things done for Abbey Wood:

**Cleaning up Wilton Road**
_We'll tackle anti-social behaviour on Wilton Road and right across Abbey Wood - fining those who litter, cracking down on betting shops when they break the rules, and making sure women and girls feel safe._
 
**Standing up to Rogue Landlords**
_We'll take strong enforcement action against rogue landlords with £40,000 fines - including those who mismanage HMOs and exploit Abbey Wood residents._

**Clean Streets and Reliable Bin Collections**
_We'll keep Abbey Wood clean and well-maintained - proactively removing fly-tipping, and keeping trees and hedges along our roads and in green spaces like Bostall Woods and Abbey Wood Park protected. We’ll make sure your bins are collected on time, every time._

Across Greenwich we are also promising to:

- Deliver 6000 social and low rent homes over the next decade 🏡

- Tackle fly tipping and cut the bulky waste collection fee to £5 🚮

- Provide a £150 voucher for every child and young person aged 16 and under in the Borough to help them buy a bike or scooter and safety equipment 🚲

- Build a new SEND school for local children 📚

- Crack down on crime and anti-social behaviour by expanding our ASB enforcement team 🚨

**Vote Labour on 7 May to make Abbey Wood the place to be** 🌹

At the next General Election, the people of Lewisham East can make a choice, do they want to vote for a party (Labour, Conservatives, Liberal Democrats and Greens) whose leaders have signed up for the United Nations and World Economic Forum’s, Agenda 2030, ‘Build Back Better’ and ‘Great Reset’ agendas. Or do they want a party who will do what the voters have asked for.

The reason I voted for Brexit was to reclaim British sovereignty. ReformUK want to reject the influence of the World Economic Forum and cancel membership of the World Health Organisation. They also oppose the creation of the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), which could stop us gaining access to our bank accounts, or tell us we have a certain amount of time to spend the money in our bank account, or we would lose it. The globalists also want to impose 15 minute cities on us and restrict our freedom to travel. At this election we have an opportunity to fight for our freedoms.

As a teacher I would like to see smart phones banned in schools. Young people are becoming addicted to their phones and have very little attention span. This effects their learning. Phones can be used to bully their peers and adults can use it to push things like transgender ideology and Critical Race Theory on vulnerable young people. ReformUK wants to deal with this by launching inquiries into these issues.

I am also concerned about the rise in knife crime and crime in general in London. ReformUK want to increase the number of police and get police back to fighting crime, rather than policing comments made on social media and promoting the woke agenda.

ReformUK also want to set up and Excess Deaths and Vaccine Harms Public Inquiry. During Covid people were given false information about the Covid vaccines and pressurised into taking the vaccine by the government and mainstream media. Eminent doctors who spoke out about the dangers of these vaccines were censored and were painted as ‘crazy anti-vaxers’ and accused of sprea

I am a climate campaigner, writer and editor who has lived in Blackheath most of the last 30 years. For the last three I have been campaigning with the Stop the Silvertown Tunnel Coalition and Extinction Rebellion.

The 4-lane Silvertown Tunnel will take bigger HGVs than Blackwall and push more congestion past homes and schools. Kidbrooke will be affected. Research in Greenwich and nearby boroughs by Kings College London shows the lungs of children aged 8-9 who breathe polluted air are approximately 5% smaller than they should be.

Instead, let's have a network of low traffic zones throughout the borough. And a range of actions to encourage cycling, walking, cargo bikes and public transport: e.g. extension of London's bike hire, DLR to Eltham and Thamesmead, a cycle/ pedestrian bridge at the Thames Barrier, good public transport links to the new Elizabeth Line.

Climate crisis and air pollution, global and local, need addressing urgently in a Green New Deal. At global level I see how climate change already affects subsistence communities in semi-arid Africa (through an NGO I'm involved with that supports their brilliant work to store water in seasonal river-beds). Locally I have joined a new group of residents taking action on dirty air and dangerous driving, because the council and police just won't. The trees I got for my street are (late March) coming into blossom.

Greens are rising around here - if you are longing for bold action for a greener Greenwich, give us your vote on 5 May. Help get the first ever Green voices onto the council.

I am proud to be a Greenwich resident. As a consultant at the intersection of Science and Business, with a degree in Biochemistry and an MBA, I bring evidence-based thinking and a practical, results-driven approach to everything I do.

Over the last decade I have worked (in a voluntary capacity) in Harm Reduction, supporting people with addiction issues and developing programmes to minimise the risks associated with substance use. I regularly organise and lead groups of volunteers for welfare support at festivals and events.

I am actively involved in the Greenwich community, also volunteering with the Woolwich Service Users Project (WSUP) and other initiatives for vulnerable people, helping them connect with services that improve their wellbeing and stability.

As a candidate for council, my priorities are protecting our green spaces, championing local independent shops and maintaining cleaner, safer neighbourhoods. My professional work across international contexts has shown me how quickly public trust erodes when governments lack transparency. I strongly believe accountability starts at the ground level, and I would strive to ensure the council is efficient and answerable to the people it serves.

Greens are rising around here - if you are longing for bold action for a greener Greenwich, give us your vote on the 7th of May.

I was Shooters Hill born and bred and care deeply about this ward, its environment and its residents. I do not want to see our ward spoiled by the sell off of our community assets, plagued by the imposition of an unnecessary Controlled Parking Zone or defiled by a glut of HMOs or the development of precious brownfield sites. I promise to listen to residents' concerns, take them seriously and support them in the Town Hall to gain resolution.

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt:
I have lived in Greenwich since 2022. I believe everyone has the right to live well, hope for the future and have access to decent housing and clean, safe transport. I’m passionate that young people shouldn’t have to leave the borough to find housing and raise families, and I support a tax on the wealthiest to pay for free universal childcare.

I have worked for over a decade to improve access to education, art, archives and libraries, managing projects and fundraising for charities around the world, including in Jordan and the Occupied Palestinian Territories. I want to use these skills to work with the community to make Charlton Hornfair a better place for all its residents.

I am working with local campaigners to push Greenwich council Pension Fund to divest from arms companies linked to war crimes committed in Gaza and Lebanon.

I am proud to have been chosen as the Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for Bexleyheath and Crayford.   I have lived in South East London since 1995. I recently retired from the Foreign Office, after a career in foreign affairs and development assistance that took me to Africa, Asia and Central and Eastern Europe. I served as Ambassador for the UK and for the European Union. I am an active member of the Greenwich and Bexley Green Party. I stood as a candidate in the 2022 local elections, and I have been engaging regularly with my local Council to push for a better performance on housing, environment and other issues. I am standing for Parliament because I want to be part of the Green Party's efforts to change the policy agenda and to make a positive difference. ​​​​​​​

Paul, a lifelong borough resident, knows every knook and cranny of the area. Raised in a working-class family, he excelled in secondary and further education, becoming a qualified surveyor. Now semi-retired, he works part-time after a successful, semi-employed carer. Sociable, courteous, and unbiased, Paul treats everyone fairly. He joined Reform UK, passionately believing it's the only party truly representing UK citizens. Standing as a candidate, he aims to give locals a real chance to vote for meaningful change and a brighter future.

I’m Christian Achilli, a local resident standing for Eltham Park & Progress. I’ve built my career as a senior technology architect, working with global organisations to solve complex problems and deliver practical results. I want to bring that same experience, accountability and fresh thinking to our community. I care about keeping our streets safe, improving local services and ensuring residents get real value for their council tax. I believe in listening to local people and standing up for our area. I’m standing with Reform UK to offer a positive, common-sense approach and to put our community first.

Ben married recently and works as a solicitor in London, and also volunteers his time as a trustee of local charities.
He says: "We know from speaking to people across Eltham Park and the Progress Estate just how badly let down they feel.  Whether its the potholes that plague our roads, Sadiq Khan's cuts to policing or the Council's parking charge increases that are hurting businesses and residents alike - Labour are letting Eltham down.  I'm looking forward to working with Anthonia to offer a positive alternative at the local elections May, and working as hard as I can to give residents a voice."

A professional Human Resource  practitioner of over two decades.   Highly experienced managing people and resources. A strong advocate for my local community.  As a board member of a big Greenwich based charity for families with disabled children, provided free HR and management services to the charity.  Volunteers in another global charity raising funds to support children in over a 100 countries to be in education, stay safe and healthy. Passionate about helping youths in their career choices and empowering them through skills acquisition UK government sponsored employment programs.  Provides work experience platforms for young adults.  Family oriented and speak up against domestic violence.
Will be the strong voice for Eltham Park and Progress community if elected to:
1. Campaign for more Police Officers on our streets to cut down crime, anti-social behaviour, protect families and children.
2. Press for stronger legal protections for family homes from HMOs
3. Better street cleaning and waste removal
4. Protect local business 
5. Fix potholes for safety of motorist and cyclists
6. Advocate facelift of our Eltham Parks and gardens for the enjoyment of the community.
7. Advocate for development in Eltham as done in Woolwich.

If elected, pledges to serve Eltham Park and Progress with integrity and commitment to the community.  Please Vote Anthonia Adolphus-Ugo on 7th May 2026 for a strong voice in Eltham Park and Progress, Greenwich

Thank you.

The first house I lived in was in Lannoy Road in New Eltham, I grew up by Eltham Station and now my partner, Alice, and I are raising our family on Archery Road.

I’m looking forward to speaking to as many residents as possible over the next month on the issues that are important to our community. It’s crucial that we do more to support the High Street and our other shopping parades, protect and improve our wonderful green spaces, and make the ward safer - particularly after Sadiq Khan’s disastrous decision to close Eltham Police Station.

Well,as an Arnold I promised in the the general election and the by election "I'll be back"

And I am back

I was initially asked to stand in the General Election by constituents who saw what I have been undertaking to improve Eltham.

I am a professional Fellow of the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors, an expert witness in Landlord and Tenant and building disputes and recognised by media as having the expertise to regularly be consulted on TV News, radio news, Panorama and in the press, I lead by example,

I get my hands dirty, I paint the bollards, posts, kerbs that have been neglected, I clean the dirt off the streets, I remove the graffiti..... I plant up neglected areas.  To check out what I have been up to, and see how others are now joining in, check out "We Love Eltham" on Facebook.

Whilst others offer more of the same - more cuts, worse services, I offer you something different.  If we want change then WE the Residents of Eltham must take back control, exceed our authority and build a Community, Build Hope, Restore Faith and improve our Well Being.

TOGETHER WE CAN ACHIEVE

Whilst others only offer more cuts in an ever downward spiral WE can leave a legacy to be proud of. I cannot do this on my own - I need YOU to become Revolutionary in a Positive Uplifting way and to join me in fighting back against years of cuts and neglect.

To find out more about me Google Arnold Tarling, Lakanal House, Ledbury Estate, Grenfell.  I have been campaigning for building safety for years - not only in matters reported in the media.

I am a local Activist working for YOU even though you have never asked me to do so.and even though I am unpaid  AND:- IF a miracle occurs and I were to be elected rest assured that I will be entirely different to any councillor you have had previously.

I will continue to work to improve OUR Eltham whether elected or not.

My work is for Local issues, NOT national or international issues.  Local Councils are about Local Issues and I will

Stacy Smith is co-coordinator of Greenwich and Bexley Green Party and CEO of Her Centre, a Greenwich woman's advocacy service, which works to help women get free from abusive people and situations. Originally from the United States, Stacy has lived and worked in Greenwich for over 30 years. She previously ran for election for Greenwich Council for East Greenwich ward, coming within 57 votes of being the first ever Green councillor in our area.

Too many voices in our ward go unheard. The average resident spends half their income on rent, yet private and social rents continue to rise. Taylor Wimpey will make £420 million in profits while building luxury apartments in our ward that no-one can afford. Thames Water reported a £328 million profit in the first half of 2025/26, as the Greenwich Pumping Station regularly discharges sewage.
I’m tired of being told what’s “best” by people who have made money buying our houses and our water, while those same people ignore minority groups, rent out third, fourth or fifth homes, and enforce inaction. Enough is enough.

My name is Denise Scott-McDonald and I am your Labour parliamentary candidate for the constituency of Sevenoaks for the next General election. First, I would like to say a huge thank you to everyone, I have met so far, who has made me feel so at home.

Here is a little bit about myself, I am a former deputy Leader and currently a cabinet lead, in the south London borough of Greenwich. An area that has many of the similar characteristics of Sevenoaks and Swanley, it has areas of affluence and pockets of both unseen and seen poverty, and at the same time, it's residents are facing some of the same challenges, like the cost of living crisis, a local NHS that is under enormous pressures, and a lack of affordable housing. I want to fight to change this.

Also Sevenoaks and Swanley are experiencing regeneration and growth, which again is similar to an area I currently represent. I know what it takes to bring key stakeholders together to fight for investment and deliver the changes that people want. I am used to working at both the strategic and local level to challenge and bring positive change to an area experiencing growth.

I am used to working in and winning a challenging seat. Since 2014, I have been a ward councillor in the marginal ward, a seat that have been targeted by the main opposition in that area. Having the experience, skillsets and resilience to fight a challenging seat will be important to fighting for Labour in this part of Kent and I believe I have it.

I am a strong and active Labour campaigner. Since 2008, I have worked on all the General Elections, and London mayoral, Europeans and local council election campaigns for the Labour Party, as well as numerous by-elections. Also I have been the CLP Secretary and held numerous positions at both constituency and branch level.

Outside of local government, I am the President of the National Members Council (NMC) for the Coop Group, a national position within the cooperative movement. In the role, I chair a 1

I served as a local Councillor in Greenwich for twenty eight years. I chaired various committees during that time. And was vice chair of the Licensing and Woolwich & Thamesmead planning committees also.
I was twice Mayor of Greenwich. I am a retired Actor who appeared in Eastenders , The Bill and many other programmes. Prior to acting I was a Registered Dockworker and a Shop Steward and Trade Union Lay Delegate for many years.
I also contribute articles for a National magazine. 
I've lived in Greenwich for fifty seven years and reside in Shooters Hill Rd in Kidbrooke Park ward.

I'm proud to be from Kidbrooke Village and Sutcliffe. I grew up on Meadowcourt Road and went to school at Wingfield then Thomas Tallis. This area shaped who I am today and I want to give back as a hard working, engaged and proactive councillor. I've spoken with hundreds of local people and they've told me they want to protect and invest in our green spaces, tackle crime and anti-social behaviour and make sure their voice is heard by our council. That's what I'll focus on as your councillor.

Recent polls (PollCheckUK) show that Kidbrooke Village and Sutcliffe is a 2-horse race between Labour and Reform UK. If you want local, hardworking councillors and to stop Reform. Use both votes for Labour.

I'm running for councillor with the Green Party because I want us all to have better lives. I'm a local mother of two school-age kids, and I want to do what I can for people like me who are facing multiple challenges.

Some of the issues I want to work for you to improve in Kidbrooke Village and Sutcliffe:

1. Ensure new homes are truly affordable homes - which includes reasonable service charges
2. Challenge the planning permissions for non-stop developments, particularly on our green spaces
3. Challenge the budget, as council taxes rise whilst services are reduced (or services are put on developers, who pass on the costs to leaseholders). This includes ensuring a sustainable approach to fly tipping and SEND provision.
4. Ensure safety on our roads, including addressing lack of parking for developments leading to illegal or parking issues on surrounding roads

I live in Greenwich and have been part of the borough for over 45 years. I was educated at Crown Woods School and have previously worked in Avery Hill.
I have a long history of community and campaign work locally, with a strong record of achieving practical outcomes for residents. My work has included reporting environmental issues such as fly‑tipping and graffiti, challenging inappropriate planning applications, and consistently championing local concerns.
I was a founding member of a building preservation trust that successfully prevented Severndroog Castle from being sold off by the council, helping to safeguard an important local heritage site for public benefit.
I have also worked with the environmental charity Thames21 on community engagement projects to improve Gallions Lake, and I ran a river action group that removed tonnes of plastic waste from the Thames foreshore at Crossness. Alongside this, I have actively lobbied for better walking and cycling access across the borough and regularly attended council meetings to ask questions and hold decision‑makers to account.
I am committed to representing the interests of residents in Middle Park and Horn Park and to progressing the issues that matter most to the local community. For real hope and real change, vote for the Green Party on 7 May 2026.

I was raised and educated in Bexley, where my roots run deep. As a qualified teacher and educationalist, I've had the privilege of working in both the state and independent sectors, making significant contributions to the field of education, particularly in computer science. This diverse professional background has given me a unique perspective on the educational system and the vital reforms it requires.

In the 2019 General Election, I stood as the Brexit Party candidate for the Erith & Thamesmead constituency, achieving the third-largest share of the vote for our party in London. My campaign was driven by a passionate advocacy for Brexit and addressing local issues that matter to our community.

Continuing my political journey with Reform UK, I am now standing as their candidate in the Bexleyheath & Crayford constituency for the upcoming General Election. My campaign is focused on significant reforms in governance and public spending, reflecting my commitment to tackling both local and national issues with innovative solutions. My dedication to public service is evident in my consistent efforts to engage with and represent the people of our area.

I'm very active on social media, where I connect with the community and share my political views. You can find more information about my campaign and policies on the Reform UK website and my social media channels. Through my continued dedication and involvement in politics, I aim to bring about positive changes that resonate with the needs and aspirations of the people in our community.

My name is Charles. I’ve lived in the Royal Borough of Greenwich for almost 20 years. As a Councillor, my focus would be on reducing Council waste and reversing falling standards. I’ll fight to ensure that our Council serves normal, local people and always puts them first. Family. Community. Country.

As a writer and local campaigner, I believe in the power of politics to change lives. I am passionate about supporting residents who are already doing fantastic things for the area — strengthening your initiatives, celebrating your successes, and creating hope through shared enjoyment and endeavour. Drawing on my experience in family business and academia, I will seek to give you more power over your neighbourhood, channelling your concerns into vibrant campaigns, while nourishing a stronger sense of community pride, creativity and belonging.

Taxation should be an investment in our community, not a burden for failing services. I will work to eliminate council waste & redirect those funds to what matters. You deserve to feel safe, happy, and respected by a Council that spends your money wisely to create a cleaner, safer, better Greenwich.

I’m a dedicated social worker and mother of two, with years of experience supporting families across the NHS, community, and voluntary sectors. I understand the real challenges residents face, from housing and healthcare to inequality, and I know how to get results. I’ve delivered frontline support during COVID-19, volunteered with the Red Cross, and worked closely with local communities in Greenwich. I’m running for council to be a strong voice for you – championing better housing, safer communities, opportunities for young people, and equal rights for all. I will listen, act, and fight to ensure every resident is heard and supported.

Through local activism and volunteer work, giving legal support and housing advice to vulnerable groups after years of Tory austerity, I’ve developed a strong belief in the importance of care in our communities. Studying History and Politics in Greenwich has strengthened my understanding of public policy, governance and community representation. As someone who relies on council services myself, I understand how vital they are to daily life and am committed to promoting accountable, responsive and well-resourced local government that delivers effectively for residents.

I have lived in Greenwich all my life. I brought up my children here and they’ve stayed too. I have helped with many environmental campaigns locally. I care deeply about the environment and am a keen gardener. An artist and teacher, I have taught English to asylum seekers and refugees. I understand what drove them to escape the life threatening circumstances they faced.

My attachment to my home borough means I care about the conservation of historic Greenwich and feel strongly about protecting the local environment from over development. I have been a Charlton fan all my life.

I am an artist, teacher, and environmental campaigner, and have lived in Greenwich since 1993. I brought up three children here, who all live locally, and we love living in Greenwich. I am passionate about the environment and protecting natural habitats. My studio is on Deptford Creek and I have seen at first hand water levels rising, and how climate change already affects both the human and the natural world in London, as well its threat to us all globally. I want a big Green Party presence on the council to help us address it and prepare.

I am so proud of myself and my fellow candidates right now! 
Greenwich Green Party  Candidate:

Emma Dalmayne – Plumstead and Glyndon.

I am an Autistic mother, Activist and an Autistic rights campaigner. I have brought up six children in Plumstead over the last 26 years, and have experienced the worst and the best of the local schools in the Greenwich Borough.

I am a home educator, and I am passionate about the rights of parents being able to access support for their disabled children whether in school or out. I am the CEO of an Autistic led organisation, Autistic Inclusive Meets based in Woolwich. I am also a Community Liaison Advocate for the HER Centre in Greenwich.

I have been campaigning against fake cures, domestic violence and abusive treatments for the last ten years. I have led protests and campaigns, promoting and fighting for the rights of disabled people.

https://greenwichandbexley.greenparty.org.uk/local-elections-2026/#robdavies

Plumstead & Glyndon Manifesto

I’m standing to represent Plumstead and Glyndon because our community deserves better.

- Protect family homes and improve housing standards
- Make our streets safer with stronger local policing
- Support residents through the cost of living crisis
- Protect and improve our parks and green spaces

I am not a career politician—I will listen, work hard, and act for residents.

Stewart Christie has lived beside Plumstead Common for over 20 years and has raised his two children here.

Passionate about local democracy, and the obvious neglect of our area, he will stand up for all Plumstead Common residents and actively engage in forensic scrutiny of the council leadership, systems, and structures.

I have lived in the area since 2010 and raised my 3 daughters here. I'm an actor/voice actor but with a history of over 20 years work in the social sector with young people, in the realms of anti social behaviour, homelessness & drugs and alcohol.

As a leader of the successful campaign to save the Plumstead Adventure Play Centre, I saw firsthand how the council operates and it shocked me. I'm standing to change that: for a council that listens, takes responsibility and puts residents first

Tamasin Rhymes lives with her family in the ward, is a school governor and a highly respected and engaged member of the community. 
Tamasin says:

“It is vital to protect our community facilities, which need security of tenure, and not to have bits of it sold off piece by piece. To deal with anti social behaviour and knife crime we desperately need more safe spaces and youth centres for our young people. The Cubs and Scouts, who provide exactly this, need a secure home and the property at Shrewsbury House, which the council agreed to sell off, would be a potential venue.”

“The council offers no meaningful community engagement on the environmental issues we face. Projects like Herbert Road Green Oasis have found that the council failed to work with them despite previous promises. Fly tipping is an issue we need to work hard on. And Herbert Road is a problem area for shoplifting and the businesses there needs more careful support to alleviate crime. The council sits on large amounts of community infrastructure levy money which should be set aside for areas like this.”

“Another major issue is that so much house building is going on in the borough which doesn’t cater for existing residents who need at least affordable housing, while the percentage of social housing in new builds remains shockingly low.”

Tamasin Rhymes manages Greener and Cleaner’s community Hub in The Glades shopping Centre, Bromley, and is a member of Community Trade Union. She is co-founder and Chair of non party collective Greener Greenwich Community Network which aims to improve our climate resilience through education and community events. Tamasin was part of the creation of Plum Lane Community Orchard and Plumcroft’s Eco Council work as well working on biodiversity and food growing across the borough.

The by-election was caused by the resignation of Shooters Hill Councillor Ivis Williams from the Labour Party over the treatment of her by the Labour majority council when she defended the concer

I’m a local activist and previously worked in the charity sector, training young people as camera operators and journalists across the UK. I now run a video production company in the borough, and always prioritise independent businesses, freelancers and investment in our local economy.

I’ve lived in Greenwich for 15 years, with most of that time renting in an HMO in Charlton, so I know first-hand the kind of damage that expensive and insecure housing does to your health. **Labour have controlled Greenwich Council since 1971. It’s time for independent voices that put people and planet over party**.

I have lived and worked in Greenwich for over thirty years and, if elected, will use my local knowledge on behalf of the voters in Shooters Hill. I am passionate about protecting our environment here on the hill and promoting a fair deal for all residents.

Labour has taken voters for granted in Greenwich. Concerns about overcrowded buses, planning decisions, as well as cuts to children's centres and long waiting lists for hospital appointments can not be ignored. As a Liberal Democrat I will listen to residents and work on their behalf to challenge Labour to do better.

**Meet Ezra Aydin, your Conservative Candidate for West Thamesmead**

As a father of two young children, I am deeply invested in the future, safety, and prosperity of our community.

**My Background & Values**
Born in South Africa with a mixed heritage, I have been fortunate to experience a rich diversity of cultures from which I have developed a profound respect for, and practice of, core traditional British values ~ values of hard work, community resilience, and civic responsibility.

Professionally, I bring a strong track record in economic policy and foreign investment. I served as a Political and Economic Advisor in China’s Chengdu Hi-Tech zone (similar to a municipal borough), helping guide major cultural and investment landmarks like the _Tianfu Twin Towers_ with luscious architecture complimented by clean parks and open spaces for all residents. I had also advised Central Governments in South East Asia on economic restructuring, securing foreign investment in infrastructure and agriculture while navigating complex European trade policies they faced. I know exactly what it takes to attract serious investment and create sustainable jobs, and I want to bring that expertise directly to our borough.

**My Pledges to West Thamesmead**
I am committed to tackling the core issues our residents are suffering with. If elected, I will focus on:

》〉Zero Tolerance on Crime: Residents deserve to feel safe in their homes and walking along the Thames Path! I will fight for a strict, zero-tolerance approach against criminals to tackle the burglaries, vehicle thefts, and violent crime affecting our ward.

》〉Driving Investment and Job Creation: Drawing on my background in international economic development, I will actively work to attract new business and local investment to Greenwich, generating real, high-quality opportunities and jobs for our residents.

》〉Enhancing and Protecting Green Spaces: We must preserve the unique beauty of West Thamesmead. I will push to create vast

West Thamesmead deserves change.

For over 50 years, the Conservative Party has been in opposition in Greenwich, while the Labour Party has led the Royal Borough of Greenwich. For too long, one party has dominated our local politics and our community has been left without the progress it deserves.

This election on 7 May is not a contest between Labour and the rest it is a real opportunity for change.

A vote for Reform UK risks dividing that change. Figures like Nigel Farage may dominate headlines, but West Thamesmead needs serious, local leadership focused on our community not national distractions.

Here in West Thamesmead, Labour has held power for the past 16 years. During that time, residents have seen rising concerns about crime and anti-social behaviour, as well as ongoing issues with littering, pollution and rise in council tax. Our community deserves better and it deserves to be heard.

This election will be close. Every vote will matter.

I am standing to represent you because I believe West Thamesmead can do better. Since moving here in 2017, I have built my life in this community. I studied law at the University of Westminster, gaining knowledge in housing, employment, family, immigration, and human rights law. These are not just subjects, and they are real issues that affect people’s daily lives.

If elected, I will bring that knowledge, commitment, and determination to serve every resident of West Thamesmead. I will work to ensure your concerns are taken seriously and that our area finally gets the attention and improvement it deserves.

On 7 May, vote for change. Vote for a stronger future for West Thamesmead.
